question_text: On a wave diagram, what is the name of the highest point and the lowest point on a transverse wave?

options.label: A
options.text: Highest point = crest, lowest point = trough

options.label: B
options.text: Highest point = amplitude, lowest point = wavelength

options.label: C
options.text: Highest point = frequency, lowest point = compression

options.label: D
options.text: Highest point = peak, lowest point = amplitude

explanation: The highest point of a transverse wave is called a crest (or peak), and the lowest point is called a trough. The distance between them is twice the amplitude.
